task_name: Earthquake Impact Assessment in Myanmar using Daily NTL (2025)
description: Assess the impact of the 2025 Mw7.7 Mandalay earthquake on urban power infrastructure. The workflow retrieves official earthquake parameters (USGS/ReliefWeb), defines multi-scale epicenter buffers (25 km, 50 km, and 100 km), and utilizes the GEE Python API to compare daily VNP46A2 radiance across three critical periods: pre-event baseline, the first post-event overpass night, and the post-disaster recovery phase.

input.query: 2025 Myanmar earthquake official USGS ReliefWeb epicenter magnitude event time

steps.description: 1. Parse event date/time (UTC), epicenter coordinates, and magnitude from Step 1 official sources. 2. Convert event time to epicenter local time and determine the first post-event overpass night for VNP46A2. Do not assume a fixed 02:00 local overpass; use a candidate window such as 00:30-02:30 local and narrow it with pixel-level UTC_Time or official metadata when the UTC date boundary matters. Use GEE VNP46A1.UTC_Time only when VNP46A1 covers the target date; for recent events beyond GEE VNP46A1 coverage, use LAADS/CMR granule timing or official metadata. If the event occurs after that nightly overpass on day D, use day D+1 as the local first-night label (not D). Then convert the selected local first-night acquisition time or candidate range back to UTC before choosing any UTC-indexed image/file date. For the Myanmar 2025 event, a plausible local acquisition range around 2025-03-29 00:30-02:30 MMT maps to about 2025-03-28 18:00-20:00 UTC, so a UTC-indexed first-night image/file date is 2025-03-28. 3. Initialize GEE and define three epicenter buffers: 25 km, 50 km, and 100 km. 4. Load 'NASA/VIIRS/002/VNP46A2' daily radiance data and compute spatial mean ANTL for each buffer across three windows: pre-event baseline (event-14d to event-7d), first post-event overpass night, and post-event recovery (event+7d to event+14d). 5. Save period-level statistics to 'outputs/Myanmar_Earthquake_ANTL_2025.csv' with fields: buffer_km, period, start_date, end_date, mean_antl, and document both local_first_night_date and utc_file_date in the run notes/report.
steps.name: geospatial_code_step_2

steps.description: Read 'outputs/Myanmar_Earthquake_ANTL_2025.csv' and compute blackout impact metrics dynamically for each buffer (e.g., drop_pct = (event_night - baseline) / baseline * 100). In the report, explicitly document the first-night selection rule, the local first post-event overpass date/time, the corresponding UTC acquisition time, and the UTC image/file date used for querying. Generate a structured JSON impact report in 'outputs/Impact_Assessment_Report.json' including assumptions, formulas, per-buffer computed values (25/50/100 km), cross-buffer comparisons, and cited official event metadata from Step 1.
steps.name: geospatial_code_step_3

task_name: Iran conflict nightlight impact assessment using official VJ102/VJ103
description: Assess conflict-related nighttime light changes in Iran with official-source VJ102DNB/VJ103DNB daily products. The workflow runs official full-chain preprocessing, refreshes INSS strike points, and rebuilds the analysis report.

steps.description: Run tools/fetch_inss_arcgis_strikes.py with --end-date aligned to analysis window to refresh latest strike points and spatial summaries.
steps.name: refresh_inss_points

steps.description: Run base_data/Iran_War/analysis/scripts/modular_refactor/rebuild_iran_event_report.py to regenerate combined report with updated rasters and strike points.
steps.name: rebuild_conflict_report

_evolution.reason: Add reusable event-impact workflow for official VJ102/VJ103 conflict analysis and report rebuild.

task_name: Iran conflict local-topic mapping with official VJ146A1 QA-controlled Tehran and Hormuz views
description: Generate Tehran and Strait of Hormuz local topic maps and GIFs from official VJ146A1 daily products. The workflow uses the formal fullchain tool in gridded mode with explicit qa_mode, prepares case-specific Tehran map inputs in the workflow layer, and renders two white-basemap viridis outputs without hardcoding these presets into generic tools.

steps.description: Read `base_data/Iran_War/data/boundaries/iran_geoboundaries_all_levels_shp/iran_geoboundaries_adm2.shp` and extract the Tehran polygon to `/data/processed/workflow_cases/q22_iran_vj146a1_tehran_hormuz/map_inputs/tehran_boundary.geojson`. Then read `base_data/Iran_War/data/event_feeds/inss_arcgis_strikes_latest/inss_arcgis_strikes_merged.geojson`, clip recent events within the Tehran display window for 2026-02-25..2026-03-05, and save `/data/processed/workflow_cases/q22_iran_vj146a1_tehran_hormuz/map_inputs/tehran_recent_strikes.geojson`.
steps.name: prepare_tehran_case_inputs

_evolution.reason: Move Tehran/Hormuz case presets into workflow guidance instead of embedding regional assumptions into generic tools.
